title: Minimum Number of Flips to Make Binary Grid Palindromic I
content:
You are given an m x n binary matrix grid.

A row or column is considered palindromic if its values read the same forward and backward.

You can flip any number of cells in grid from 0 to 1, or from 1 to 0.

Return the minimum number of cells that need to be flipped to make either all rows palindromic or all columns palindromic.

Example 1:

Input: grid = [[1,0,0],[0,0,0],[0,0,1]]

Output: 2

Explanation: Flipping the highlighted cells makes all the rows palindromic.

Example 2:

Input: grid = [[0,1],[0,1],[0,0]]

Output: 1

Explanation: Flipping the highlighted cell makes all the columns palindromic.

Example 3:

Input: grid = [[1],[0]]

Output: 0

Explanation: All rows are already palindromic.

Constraints:

• m == grid.length

• n == grid[i].length

• 1 <= m * n <= 2 * 105

• 0 <= grid[i][j] <= 1
hints: We need to perform the operation only when the equivalent element of i from the back is not equal.

solutions.explanation:
We compute the cost to make ALL rows palindromic and the cost to make ALL columns palindromic, then return the minimum.

For rows: for each row r, count pairs (c, n-1-c) where values differ — each needs 1 flip.
For columns: for each column c, count pairs (r, m-1-r) where values differ — each needs 1 flip.

Time: O(m*n). Space: O(1).
